creator: avg@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Konstantin Belousov from comment #11)
Perhaps Tx that is keeping its reference.
I don't know what kind of code people can write to manipulate mbuf, this is just a theoretical example.

Larger point is why have the special case, does it provide a lot of win for performance?
I know that atomic operations are expensive. A branch is not free, however, too.
Always doing the atomic update is straightforward and safe(r).
